source: User-supplied published GTO chart, transcribed via cell-by-cell comparison in docs/preflop_100bb_chart_validation_2026-05-28.md (v1) and docs/preflop_100bb_chart_validation_v2_2026-05-28.md (v2). Provenance of the originating chart is not labeled in the validation doc.
license_notes: User-supplied chart; provenance not labeled. If the user identifies this as a commercial-solver export (GTOW / Pio / Snowie), this fixture should be moved to a non-public location pending licensing review (per docs/blueprint_reference_inventory.md §5).

config.action_menu_notes: Chart published with 2.5bb open, 10bb 3-bet, 23bb 4-bet. Engine equivalents (closest discrete sizes) are 2.5bb / 8.5bb / 22.3bb. Sizing mismatch of ~1.5bb at 3-bet and ~0.7bb at 4-bet contributes to documented divergence. Chart does NOT include a SB-limp action; engine's full action set does. See validation doc §'View B' (limp-collapsed view) for the chart-aligned categorization.
config.convention_notes: Engine uses Brown convention for terminal-utility (winner gets pot+c_loser; see feedback_brown_convention_adopt). Chart's underlying solver convention is unknown.

SB_RFI.expected_strategy_notes: Chart publishes dominant-action only, not full strategy distribution. expected_dominant_action below.
SB_RFI.notes: 15-cell per-cell labels reported in docs/preflop_100bb_chart_validation_2026-05-28.md table 'SB RFI cells'. Chart aggregate is fold 21.65% / open 78.35%. Marginal cells J7o, Q5o are 'fold' on this chart and 'open' on our engine — known Nash-multiplicity at 100bb (validation doc §'Top-3 Cells Where We Disagree Most' row 'J7o/Q5o'). 22 / 98s appear as 'open' on the chart and as 'limp' (call) on our engine View A; under View B (limp-collapsed) the engine matches. SB-limp absent from chart by convention.

BB_vs_SB_RFI.notes: 13-cell per-cell labels in docs/preflop_100bb_chart_validation_2026-05-28.md table 'BB vs SB RFI cells'. Chart's nominal 3-bet target is 10bb; engine's closest discrete size is 8.5bb (r850 token). Suited-ace bluff-3-bet hands (A2s, A5s) on chart vs flat-call on engine — Nash multiplicity in this region (validation doc §'Top-3 Cells' row 'A5s'). KQs flat-call on chart vs over-3-bet on engine — sizing-mismatch hypothesis. K3o defended (call) by engine vs fold on chart — chart is tighter; may close as engine converges (2500-iter run; 25k+ iters should tighten this).

SB_vs_BB_3bet.notes: 9-cell per-cell labels in docs/preflop_100bb_chart_validation_2026-05-28.md table 'SB vs BB 3-bet'. Engine matches premium-value spots (AA, KK, AKs, AKo, QQ) cleanly. A5s 4-bet bluff jam matches chart. JTs / 76s defended (call) by engine vs fold on chart — engine sees 8.5bb 3-bet as cheaper than chart's 10bb 3-bet so defense is more profitable; will likely tighten if engine is rerun with chart's exact sizing. KQs jam by engine vs call on chart — similar sizing-mismatch hypothesis.

nash_multiplicity_notes: Suited-ace bluff region (A2s / A5s / A4s at BB-vs-RFI) — chart prescribes bluff-3-bet, engine prefers flat-call. Both Nash equilibria are valid; the EV differential at 100 BB is <0.15 BB/100. Do NOT flag as a Phase 7 failure if median per-class L1 is dominated by this cluster.

Marginal SB-open cells (J7o / Q5o / similar bottom-edge offsuit hands) — chart positions them just outside opening range; engine positions them just inside. Equilibrium-selection sensitivity at 100 BB.

SB-limp asymmetry — chart prohibits limp; engine's full action set finds limp ~= open in EV. Validation doc §'View B' shows that limp-collapsing recovers chart-alignment for cells like 98s / 22. Phase 7 diff-test should report both raw and limp-collapsed views, per the validation doc precedent.

Sizing mismatch — chart calibrates at 2.5bb / 10bb / 23bb; engine's discrete menu produces 2.5bb / 8.5bb / 22.3bb. The 3-bet sizing gap (10 vs 8.5) is the largest single contributor to BB-vs-RFI divergence per the validation doc.
